title: Deal Desk Agent
Your AI Deal Desk Analyst
team_footer: Coding-agent bonus: Cursor + Claude via UiPath for Coding Agents
problem: Every sales org has a Deal Desk: the team that reviews pricing exceptions,
emails managers up the chain, waits for replies, consolidates decisions,
and updates the CRM. It is manual, inbox-driven, and slow.
- No SLA: deals stall in inboxes for days.
- No consistency: routing thresholds drift between managers.
- No audit trail: decisions buried in email threads.
solution: The Deal Desk Agent does that job, end to end on Automation Cloud:
1. Live Salesforce data via AgentHub MCP; approver chain from the org chart.
2. Risk scoring + LLM rationale -> recommendation and disposition.
3. Low-risk deals auto-approve with zero human touch.
4. WaitDecision Robot reaches each approver on 3 channels at once:
Outlook Adaptive Card + Slack DM + Action Center 'wait for task'.
5. AWS Lambda HITL bridge mints a token; any channel resolves the wait.
6. process_response interprets decisions; rejection short-circuits the chain.
7. Slack + Outlook summary to the AE; Data Fabric ApprovalAudit record.
benefits: - No chasing managers: agent routes, robot sends, BPMN tracks.
- Slack DM + Outlook summary with the full decision trail at the end.
- Low-risk deals (<=5% / <=$50k) clear with ZERO human touch.
- Consistent policy applied identically via the risk-score formula.
- Rejection short-circuits the chain (no wasted approver time).
- Data Fabric logs the agent recommendation vs. each human decision.
tech: Coded Agents (Python + LangGraph): plan, render, process_response
Maestro BPMN 2.0: sequential multi-instance loop, completionCondition
WaitDecision Robot (UiPath RPA + Persistence): all three channels
Action Center: external task 'wait for task' (Deal Desk catalog)
Outlook Adaptive Card: originator 61fed71d, Action.Http buttons, HTML fallback
Slack Block Kit: DM to approver + requester
AWS Lambda + CloudFront: HITL bridge for the approval loop
AgentHub MCP: Salesforce DealDesk MCP (live IS, read-only SOQL)
Data Fabric: ApprovalAudit; deployed to Shared/DealDeskApprovalGlobal
architecture: Salesforce event -> Maestro BPMN -> plan agent:
AgentHub MCP queries live Salesforce; org chart -> approver chain
risk_score + LLM reasoning -> RoutingPlan (disposition + rationale + chain)
auto_approve   -> Slack + Outlook to AE + Data Fabric audit (zero touch)
render agent builds the approval payload
WaitDecision Robot: Outlook Adaptive Card + Slack DM + Action Center task
AWS Lambda HITL bridge mints token; suspend via UiPath.Persistence
Approver decides on any channel -> robot resumes -> process_response
On completion: Slack DM + Outlook summary to AE -> Data Fabric ApprovalAudit
demo_body: Live test results - Jun 22 2026 (all channels verified):
TEST-003: StartupXYZ $28k/45% -> Reject (margin impact too high)
Outlook Adaptive Card | Slack DM | Action Center  - all verified
Scenario 2 - One approver (8% / $40k): Adaptive Card + Slack to Manager.
Scenario 3 - Full chain (30% / $1.2M): four sequential approvers; rejection
short-circuits; full Data Fabric audit trail.
closing: Every Deal Desk should work like this.
Collect. Reason. Route. Sign-off. Audit. Automatically.
Track 2: Maestro BPMN  |  Coding-agent bonus  |  Runs on Automation Cloud
